name: dobrinka_salzman
bio:
Dobrinka Salzman on Instagram: "As a collector and a gallery owner it is my job to understand and source the best of collectible design for my clients.
Below, Jean Prouve’s rare “Antony” chair or No 356 in a beautiful original condition, sourced for an important Japanese collection.
We truly love the hunt!
.

name: trendbark
bio:
TRENDBARK on Instagram: "COLLECTIBLE DESIGN 🛋️furniture trends

🔘 As consumers increasingly seek unique, statement-making pieces that reflect personal taste and exclusivity, these one-of-a-kind or small-batch items are a rising market trend. From limited-edition chairs and tables to exclusive decor pieces, crafted pieces by designers or artisans offer a sense of individuality in a market where mass production dominates.

🔘Owning limited-edition or collectible furniture brings a sense of rarity a

raw_data.text_sample:
TRENDBARK on Instagram: "COLLECTIBLE DESIGN 🛋️furniture trends

🔘 As consumers increasingly seek unique, statement-making pieces that reflect personal taste and exclusivity, these one-of-a-kind or small-batch items are a rising market trend. From limited-edition chairs and tables to exclusive decor pieces, crafted pieces by designers or artisans offer a sense of individuality in a market where mass production dominates.

🔘Owning limited-edition or collectible furniture brings a sense of rarity and timelessness, allowing homeowners to express their personality while investing in pieces that hold both aesthetic and potential future value. And it’s at the same time a great opportunity for designers and crafters to express themselves with creativity and uniqueness.

📍 featured / @yoomoota  is a contemporary furniture brand known for sculptures, paintings, collectible furniture, toys and NFT’s with a playful and unique taste

Discover more:
📖 LIVING+BEDROOM TRENDS 2025/26 Trendbook

#trendb

name: carawoodhouseinteriors

name: boon_room
bio:
BOON_ROOM on Instagram: "Only one day remains to come and experience the BOON_ROOM exhibit during Paris Design Week!⁠
⁠
This curated presentation brings together a diverse group of international designers and artists, each exploring the boundaries of material, form, and function through collectible design.⁠
⁠
-9 rue de Lesdiguierès, 75004 Paris⁠
⁠

name: collectiblefair
bio:
COLLECTIBLE on Instagram: "𝗔𝗱𝗼𝗿𝗻𝗼 𝗙𝗨𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘 𝟱𝟬' 𝗯𝗼𝗼𝘁𝗵 𝗮𝘁 𝗖𝗢𝗟𝗟𝗘𝗖𝗧𝗜𝗕𝗟𝗘 𝗕𝗿𝘂𝘀𝘀𝗲𝗹𝘀 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱
(@adorno.design) 

A view into FUTURE 50, Adorno’s curated stand at COLLECTIBLE Brussels 2025, brings together a striking selection of emerging voices in collectible design: the Melancholy Chair by Saturday Yard Work (@saturdayyardwork⁠) reveals gothic fluidity in cast aluminum; Studio Martina Dimitrova’s (@ma.rtin.a.di.mit.rov.a⁠) Hrisantema table reimagines Rococo florals with rhythmic surface detailing; Follow the

raw_data.text_sample:
COLLECTIBLE on Instagram: "𝗔𝗱𝗼𝗿𝗻𝗼 𝗙𝗨𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘 𝟱𝟬' 𝗯𝗼𝗼𝘁𝗵 𝗮𝘁 𝗖𝗢𝗟𝗟𝗘𝗖𝗧𝗜𝗕𝗟𝗘 𝗕𝗿𝘂𝘀𝘀𝗲𝗹𝘀 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱
(@adorno.design) 

A view into FUTURE 50, Adorno’s curated stand at COLLECTIBLE Brussels 2025, brings together a striking selection of emerging voices in collectible design: the Melancholy Chair by Saturday Yard Work (@saturdayyardwork⁠) reveals gothic fluidity in cast aluminum; Studio Martina Dimitrova’s (@ma.rtin.a.di.mit.rov.a⁠) Hrisantema table reimagines Rococo florals with rhythmic surface detailing; Follow the Flowers by Vilius Dringelis (@studiodringelis) transforms urban imprints into an aluminum bowl with layered narratives; while Pellegroms’ (@borispellegroms) WL04 wall lamp turns light into ambient sculpture through hand-beaten brass or aluminum forms.

Credit Photo: Pim Top
